text.title: Task Decomposition Principles: Size, INVEST, SMART, and Traceability
text.summary: Right-size rules (token tiers), INVEST criteria with examples, SMART applied to task goals, traceability format from task to FR-X to AD-X, and task lifecycle states.

rule.statement: Every task must fit within 100k tokens total (research + SDD docs + implementation + tests + buffer). Tasks exceeding this limit must be split at natural code boundaries.
rule.rationale: Executor agents that run out of context mid-implementation produce partial or inconsistent code. The 100k limit is a hard enforcement boundary, not a suggestion.

example.note: Token tier table for task sizing
example.code: Complexity | Token Range | Description ---------- | ----------- | ----------- simple | <30k | Single file, clear existing pattern normal | 30-60k | Multiple files, some decisions needed complex | 60-100k | Architecture decisions, research needed
Task: "Create password hashing utilities with bcrypt" (~30k tokens, single file)

rule.statement: Each task must satisfy all six INVEST criteria before being assigned to an executor. A task that fails any criterion must be rewritten or split.
rule.rationale: INVEST failures cause execution failures: non-independent tasks block each other unexpectedly, non-testable tasks cannot be verified as done.

example.note: INVEST criteria table with verification questions
example.code: Criterion | Verification Question | Example ------------ | ---------------------------------------- | ------- Independent | Can be done without blocking others? | Password utils (no deps) Negotiable | Can details be refined? | Algorithm choice is flexible Valuable | Clear user or business value? | "Enables secure login" Estimable | Can estimate tokens? | ~30k tokens Small | Fits in one work session? | Not "build auth system" Testable | Has Given-When-Then AC? | AC-1 through AC-4 defined

rule.statement: Task Objective must satisfy SMART: one interpretation only (Specific), verifiable by test (Measurable), technically feasible (Achievable), traces to FR-X (Relevant), has a token estimate (bound).
rule.rationale: Non-SMART objectives cause executors to implement the wrong scope — too much, too little, or in the wrong direction.

rule.statement: Every task must include inline FR-X and AD-X text from spec and design. Do not link — paste the full text of each requirement and decision the task implements.
rule.rationale: Executor agents that must re-read the full spec to understand their task add 10-30k tokens of overhead and risk misreading a requirement out of context.

example.code:
## Requirements Coverage
### FR-2: User can log in with email/password Full text: "System SHALL authenticate users by verifying email and bcrypt-hashed password. Returns JWT on success." This task implements: bcrypt verification in password service.
### AD-1: JWT for authentication Full text: "Use JWT with 7-day expiry, no refresh tokens for MVP. Rationale: stateless, no Redis dependency." This task implements: JWT generation and HTTP-only cookie storage.

rule.statement: Task state is determined by which directory it lives in (todo/ / in-progress/ / done/), not by the Status field in the header. The header status must always match the directory.
rule.rationale: Executor agents check the directory when scanning for work. A task in todo/ is picked up regardless of its Status header value.

list.item: todo/ — Ready to start, all dependencies met
in-progress/ — Being worked on by one executor
done/ — Completed, all AC verified, execution report filled
